metrics.name: Wale
metrics.question: Will Matthew Wale be elected as the next Prime Minister of Solomon Islands following the May 2026 no-confidence vote?

blueprint.causal_thesis: The no-confidence vote ousted PM Manele, creating a 26-member coalition that must elect a new PM. Matthew Wale, as opposition leader, is a leading candidate but faces competition from John Agovaka and potential compromise candidates due to fluid coalition politics. The outcome hinges on secret parli

terminal_canonicalization.why: vote terminals read the tally directly

pruned.reason: ceremonial: no discretion over any surviving terminal pathway

solomon_islands_coalition_instability.claim: Solomon Islands coalition governments are notoriously fluid, with frequent no-confidence motions and shifting alliances.

provenance.definition: Solomon Islands coalition governments are notoriously fluid, with frequent no-confidence motions and shifting alliances.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: In 2019, Prime Minister Manasseh Sogavare survived a no-confidence motion but later resigned amid coalition realignments.
cases.basis_quote: Solomon Islands Prime Minister Manasseh Sogavare survived a no-confidence vote in April 2019, but his coalition fractured, leading to his resignation in May 2019.

cases.description: In 2021, Prime Minister Manasseh Sogavare was ousted in a no-confidence vote, replaced by Jeremiah Manele.
cases.basis_quote: Solomon Islands Prime Minister Manasseh Sogavare was ousted in a no-confidence vote on November 24, 2021, and Jeremiah Manele was elected as his successor.

china_influence_on_solomon_islands_polit.claim: China's growing influence in the Solomon Islands affects political alignments, with pro-China and pro-Taiwan factions.

provenance.definition: China's growing influence in the Solomon Islands affects political alignments, with pro-China and pro-Taiwan factions.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: In 2019, the Solomon Islands switched diplomatic recognition from Taiwan to China, leading to political realignments.
cases.basis_quote: Solomon Islands severed ties with Taiwan and established diplomatic relations with China on September 16, 2019, a move that reshaped domestic politics.

matthew_wale.quantity: Matthew Wale is elected Prime Minister

provenance.definition: Matthew Wale, as a candidate, is formally elected by parliament and sworn in as Prime Minister.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Matthew Wale is the individual elected and sworn in.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Any other individual is elected, or no election occurs by deadline.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.basis_quote: Jeremiah Manele was elected Prime Minister on November 24, 2021, following the ouster of Manasseh Sogavare.

cases.description: In 2019, Manasseh Sogavare was re-elected Prime Minister after a no-confidence motion failed.
cases.basis_quote: Manasseh Sogavare was re-elected Prime Minister on May 15, 2019, after surviving a no-confidence vote.

outcome_reference_class.quantity: Matthew Wale is elected Prime Minister of Solomon Islands following the May 2026 no-confidence vote

provenance.definition: Matthew Wale is formally elected by parliament and sworn in as Prime Minister on or after May 7, 2026, and before August 1, 2026.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Official announcement or credible news report confirming Matthew Wale's election and swearing-in.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Any other individual elected, or no election by deadline.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: In 2021, Jeremiah Manele was elected Prime Minister after a no-confidence vote, similar process.
cases.basis_quote: Jeremiah Manele was elected Prime Minister on November 24, 2021, following a no-confidence vote.

cases.description: In 2019, Manasseh Sogavare was re-elected Prime Minister after a failed no-confidence motion.
cases.basis_quote: Manasseh Sogavare was re-elected Prime Minister on May 15, 2019.

parliament_of_solomon_islands.quorum: Majority of 50 MPs (26) must be present to elect Prime Minis
parliament_of_solomon_islands.consequence_of_nonparticipation: If no Prime Minister is elected by August 1, 2026, the Governor-General may dissolve parliament and call a general election.

weight_invariant.rule: every weight traces to a counted reference class or is explicit unknown mass; no qualitative label is mapped to a number

reversal_search.why: every actor already holds a reversal-capable state

matthew_wale.claim: Matthew Wale has privately secured ironclad commitments from at least 26 MPs to vote for him as PM, including from Frederick Kologeto's People's First Party and key independents, based on pre-negotiated portfolio allocations and policy concessions.
beliefs: Matthew Wale has privately secured ironclad commitments from at least 26 MPs to vote for him as PM, including from Frederick Kologeto's People's First Party and key independents, based on pre-negotiated portfolio allocations and policy concessions.
historical_case_refs: Solomon Islands 2019: Sogavare secured premiership via pre-vote coalition agreements
distinguishing_observations: Wale has been opposition leader for years and has deep ties with anti-government factions; his legal role in forcing the
matthew_wale.action_if_state: Wale will actively campaign as the unity candidate, publicly claim majority support, and push for an early vote to lock in his position before any defections.
latent_beliefs: Matthew Wale has privately secured ironclad commitments from at least 26 MPs to vote for him as PM, including from Frederick Kologeto's People's First Party and key independents, based on pre-negotiated portfolio allocations and policy concessions.

matthew_wale.claim: Matthew Wale believes that while he has a plurality of support, a faction within the opposition coalition (led by John Agovaka or others) is unwilling to back him, potentially due to personal rivalries or policy differences on China relations, forcing him to negotiate or step aside.
beliefs: Matthew Wale believes that while he has a plurality of support, a faction within the opposition coalition (led by John Agovaka or others) is unwilling to back him, potentially due to personal rivalries or policy differences on China relations, forcing him to negotiate or step aside.
historical_case_refs: Solomon Islands 2014: Opposition coalition fractured after no-confidence, leading to a dark horse candidate
distinguishing_observations: Agovaka is explicitly named as a candidate; Lowy Institute calls him 'most likely' from opposition, indicating Wale's po
matthew_wale.action_if_state: Wale will attempt to broker a deal with Agovaka's faction, possibly offering a deputy PM role or key ministry, while publicly maintaining his candidacy to avoid appearing weak.
latent_beliefs: Matthew Wale believes that while he has a plurality of support, a faction within the opposition coalition (led by John Agovaka or others) is unwilling to back him, potentially due to personal rivalries or policy differences on China relations, forcing him to negotiate or step aside.

john_agovaka.claim: John Agovaka has privately assembled a coalition of at least 26 MPs, including cross-benchers and defectors from the ousted government, by positioning himself as a compromise candidate less aligned with China than Wale, and more acceptable to Manele's former allies.
beliefs: John Agovaka has privately assembled a coalition of at least 26 MPs, including cross-benchers and defectors from the ousted government, by positioning himself as a compromise candidate less aligned with China than Wale, and more acceptable to Manele's former allies.
historical_case_refs: Solomon Islands 2010: Danny Philip emerged as compromise PM after no-confidence
distinguishing_observations: Lowy Institute explicitly identifies Agovaka as 'most likely PM candidate from the opposition', suggesting he has strong
john_agovaka.action_if_state: Agovaka will publicly declare his candidacy, seek to delay the vote to consolidate support, and present himself as the stable, pro-development choice to foreign diplomats.
latent_beliefs: John Agovaka has privately assembled a coalition of at least 26 MPs, including cross-benchers and defectors from the ousted government, by positioning himself as a compromise candidate less aligned with China than Wale, and more acceptable to Manele's former allies.

john_agovaka.claim: John Agovaka has decided not to run for PM himself, instead using his bloc of MPs to extract maximum concessions from either Wale or a third candidate, possibly securing the Deputy PM or Finance portfolio.
beliefs: John Agovaka has decided not to run for PM himself, instead using his bloc of MPs to extract maximum concessions from either Wale or a third candidate, possibly securing the Deputy PM or Finance portfolio.
historical_case_refs: Solomon Islands 2019: Manasseh Sogavare's rival became Deputy PM after backing down
distinguishing_observations: Agovaka's public profile is lower than Wale's; he may prefer influence over the top job, especially if he lacks the numb
john_agovaka.action_if_state: Agovaka will announce he is not a candidate, endorse Wale or another, and negotiate for key positions, while keeping his MPs united to maximize leverage.
latent_beliefs: John Agovaka has decided not to run for PM himself, instead using his bloc of MPs to extract maximum concessions from either Wale or a third candidate, possibly securing the Deputy PM or Finance portfolio.

frederick_kologeto.claim: Frederick Kologeto, as the mover of the no-confidence motion and leader of the People's First Party, holds the decisive swing votes (estimated 8-10 MPs) and has not yet committed to either Wale or Agovaka, waiting for the best offer on policy and positions.
beliefs: Frederick Kologeto, as the mover of the no-confidence motion and leader of the People's First Party, holds the decisive swing votes (estimated 8-10 MPs) and has not yet committed to either Wale or Agovaka, waiting for the best offer on policy and positions.
historical_case_refs: Solomon Islands 2007: Kingmaker party chose PM after no-confidence
distinguishing_observations: Kologeto tabled the motion, giving him procedural leverage; his party's size makes him essential for any majority
frederick_kologeto.action_if_state: Kologeto will delay endorsing any candidate, open negotiations with both camps, and demand policy commitments (e.g., on China ties, infrastructure spending) and cabinet posts for his party members.
latent_beliefs: Frederick Kologeto, as the mover of the no-confidence motion and leader of the People's First Party, holds the decisive swing votes (estimated 8-10 MPs) and has not yet committed to either Wale or Agovaka, waiting for the best offer on policy and positions.

frederick_kologeto.claim: Frederick Kologeto has already privately agreed to support a specific candidate (likely Wale or a dark horse) in exchange for the Prime Ministership for himself in a future rotation or a senior cabinet role, making the outcome predetermined.
beliefs: Frederick Kologeto has already privately agreed to support a specific candidate (likely Wale or a dark horse) in exchange for the Prime Ministership for himself in a future rotation or a senior cabinet role, making the outcome predetermined.
historical_case_refs: Solomon Islands 2014: Pre-vote deal ensured Sogavare's return
distinguishing_observations: Kologeto's decision to table the motion suggests he had a plan for succession; he may have secured a deal before the vot
frederick_kologeto.action_if_state: Kologeto will publicly endorse his chosen candidate early, rally his MPs to vote as a bloc, and work to ensure a swift election to prevent rival coalitions from forming.
latent_beliefs: Frederick Kologeto has already privately agreed to support a specific candidate (likely Wale or a dark horse) in exchange for the Prime Ministership for himself in a future rotation or a senior cabinet role, making the outcome predetermined.

coalition_mps.claim: The 26-28 opposition MPs are largely unified behind Matthew Wale as the PM candidate, with only minor dissent that will not affect the vote, due to shared anti-Manele sentiment and Wale's role in the legal challenge.
beliefs: The 26-28 opposition MPs are largely unified behind Matthew Wale as the PM candidate, with only minor dissent that will not affect the vote, due to shared anti-Manele sentiment and Wale's role in the legal challenge.
historical_case_refs: Solomon Islands 2019: Opposition united behind Sogavare after no-confidence
distinguishing_observations: The coalition was formed specifically to oust Manele, and Wale was the face of the legal effort, giving him natural lead
coalition_mps.action_if_state: MPs will vote for Wale in the first round, ensuring his election without significant delay or defection.
latent_beliefs: The 26-28 opposition MPs are largely unified behind Matthew Wale as the PM candidate, with only minor dissent that will not affect the vote, due to shared anti-Manele sentiment and Wale's role in the legal challenge.

coalition_mps.claim: The opposition coalition is deeply divided among multiple candidates (Wale, Agovaka, possibly a third), with no single candidate able to secure 26 votes initially, leading to multiple rounds of voting and potential collapse of the coalition.
beliefs: The opposition coalition is deeply divided among multiple candidates (Wale, Agovaka, possibly a third), with no single candidate able to secure 26 votes initially, leading to multiple rounds of voting and potential collapse of the coalition.
historical_case_refs: Solomon Islands 2010: Multiple rounds of voting after no-confidence
distinguishing_observations: The coalition includes six parties with diverse interests; Lowy Institute notes fluidity, and two candidates have emerge
coalition_mps.action_if_state: MPs will vote according to factional lines in initial rounds, with vote-buying and last-minute deals occurring between rounds; some may defect to the government side or abstain, prolonging the process
latent_beliefs: The opposition coalition is deeply divided among multiple candidates (Wale, Agovaka, possibly a third), with no single candidate able to secure 26 votes initially, leading to multiple rounds of voting and potential collapse of the coalition.

sample.role: Long-term Leader of the Opposition, candidate for PM
latent_beliefs: Matthew Wale has privately secured ironclad commitments from at least 26 MPs to vote for him as PM, including from Frederick Kologeto's People's First Party and key independents, based on pre-negotiated portfolio allocations and policy concessions.
public_facts: (reported) [2021-11-24] In 2021, Jeremiah Manele was elected Prime Minister after a no-confidence vote, similar process. — “Jeremiah Manele was elected Prime Minister on November 24, 2021, following a no-confidence vote.”
(reported) [2019-05-15] In 2019, Manasseh Sogavare was re-elected Prime Minister after a failed no-confidence motion. — “Manasseh Sogavare was re-elected Prime Minister on May 15, 2019.”
(confirmed) Size of opposition coalition — “The opposition coalition comprises approximately 26-28 MPs (out of 50 total parliamentary seats)”

(confirmed) [2019-04-30] Solomon Islands coalition governments are notoriously fluid, with frequent no-confidence motions and shifting alliances. — “Solomon Islands Prime Minister Manasseh Sogavare survived a no-confidence vote in April 2019, but his coalition fractured, leading to his resignation in May 2019.”

(confirmed) [2019-09-16] China's growing influence in the Solomon Islands affects political alignments, with pro-China and pro-Taiwan factions. — “Solomon Islands severed ties with Taiwan and established diplomatic relations with China on September 16, 2019, a move that reshaped domestic politics.”

(reported) [2021-11-24] In 2021, Jeremiah Manele was elected Prime Minister after a no-confidence vote ousted Manasseh Sogavare. — “Jeremiah Manele was elected Prime Minister on November 24, 2021, following the ouster of Manasseh Sogavare.”

(reported) [2019-05-15] In 2019, Manasseh Sogavare was re-elected Prime Minister after a no-confidence motion failed. — “Manasseh Sogavare was re-elected Prime Minister on May 15, 2019, after surviving a no-confidence vote.”

(reported) [2021-11-24] In 2021, Jeremiah Manele, who tabled the no-confidence motion, was elected Prime Minister. — “Jeremiah Manele, who moved the no-confidence motion, was elected Prime Minister on November 24, 2021.”

provenance.law: deliberative institution vote (D7 roster + D8 initial positions + D14 convergence + seat-weighted absolute-threshold tally); no rescaling, no independent product, no invented convergence

repairs: expanded the roster: added a seat-weighted bloc of 24 seats (large-body fidelity) — it emits a vote distribution over its seats, never one vote
diagnostics: represented voting power 2 != real body size 26 (roster collapse) — repair required, threshold must NOT be rescaled

repairs: expanded the roster: added a seat-weighted bloc of 24 seats (large-body fidelity) — it emits a vote distribution over its seats, never one vote
